title: Redundant format!+push_str can use write!
description:
Throughout the codebase, the pattern format!(...) + push_str is used to build strings incrementally. In several hot paths, std::fmt::Write + write!() would avoid intermediate String allocations:

Locations:
- src/server.rs:939-944 — personas_html.push_str(&format!(...))
- src/server.rs:1041-1045 — fields_html.push_str(&format!(...))
- src/server.rs:1056-1064 — posts_html.push_str(&format!(...))
- src/content.rs:47 — result = format!("{}{}{}", ...) — repeated string rebuilding per URL match
- src/content.rs:72-78 — same pattern for hashtag replacement
- src/content.rs:108-113 — same pattern for mention replacement
- src/theme.rs:44 — vars.push_str(&format!(...))

The content.rs string replacement pattern (lines 39-50, 64-88, 101-114) is particularly concerning: it rebuilds the entire result string for each match via format!("{}{}{}", before, replacement, after). For content with many URLs/hashtags/mentions, this is O(n*m) where n=content length and m=match count.

Impact: Unnecessary allocations. The content.rs rebuild pattern is also a correctness concern — each replacement shifts indices, making subsequent find() calls work on a mutated string, which could cause double-linking or missed matches.
close_reason: format\!+push_str replaced with write\! macro in server.rs HTML building
